channel.title: MeirLehman on RenatoTeixeira.com
channel.description: Recent content in MeirLehman on RenatoTeixeira.com

item.title: Master's Dissertation
item.description:
Today I experienced a very special moment: the defense of my master’s dissertation.

Entering the academic and scientific world close to the age of 50 was an enchanting, challenging, and deeply transformative experience. Throughout this journey, I learned so much from every professor, from my colleagues, from the examination committee, and, above all, from the opportunity to conduct research that allowed me to look at professional practice with greater method, depth, and scientific curiosity.

item.title: Legacy Does Not Happen by Accident: What Lehman Still Teaches Us About Software Evolution
item.description:
Legacy does not emerge as an isolated failure, nor as a simple consequence of age. In most important systems, it is the result of continuous adaptation under real pressures: new requirements, operational constraints, accumulated decisions, and the constant need to remain useful in a changing environment.

That is why Meir Lehman’s (1980) article remains so valuable to me. It does not treat software as a static artifact, but as something shaped by ongoing evolution. And for that reason, I believe it deserves to be much better known by those who work in the day-to-day practice of IT.
